# RustyNES v2.0.0 — "Timebase"

**RustyNES's scheduler substrate has been rewritten.** The five-counter,
dot-lockstep model (`tick_one_dot`, CPU every third dot, four other counters kept
in sync by parallel increments) is gone. In its place: **one canonical cycle
counter**, **every CPU cycle a real bus access** (no more "busless" filler
cycles), and a **split-around-the-access PPU catch-up** (`Cpu::start_cycle` /
`Cpu::end_cycle` bracket every bus access with a PPU advance, mirroring Mesen2's
`StartCpuCycle → Read → EndCpuCycle` structure). This is the **one-clock,
every-cycle-bus-access timebase** — the collapse `to-dos/plans/v2.0.0-master-clock-plan.md`
and ADR 0002 committed to, and it is now the *only* scheduler path in the
codebase (ADR 0029). **AccuracyCoin holds 100% (139/139)** on the shipped default,
`nestest` is 0-diff, and the determinism contract (same seed + ROM + input ⇒
bit-identical framebuffer and audio) holds throughout.

This is RustyNES's designated **MAJOR-boundary release** (ADR 0003): save-state
and TAS-movie compatibility across the v1.x ⇒ v2.0.0 line is **best-effort by
policy, and this specific release exercises that clause** (ADR 0028). A
pre-v2.0.0 `.rns` save-state slot now fails to load with a clear
`SnapshotError::VersionMismatch` instead of silently reinterpreting stale bytes.

---

## The rewrite, beta by beta (PRs #217–223)

- **beta.1 — the one-clock counter collapse** (behind a default-off feature
  flag). `LockstepBus::cycle` becomes the single per-cycle authority;
  `Cpu::cycles`, `Apu::cpu_cycle`, and `Cpu::master_clock` are now *derived*
  from it rather than independently incremented. A new regression pin
  (`one_clock_invariants`) proves the three legacy counters' residues against
  the canonical one are frame-over-frame constant — `(12, 0, 0)` on the shipped
  default.
- **beta.2 — every-cycle-bus-access, the STOP-OR-GO gate.** Every CPU
  instruction cycle now performs a real bus access — the historical
  **9,795-cycle busless surface measured across AccuracyCoin, nestest, and the
  full official + unofficial opcode space collapses to zero**. This closes the
  structural gap Session-18's original IRQ-timing diagnosis identified
  (RustyNES read *before* ticking the PPU; Mesen2 splits the PPU advance around
  the access) — without yet fixing the specific bug that diagnosis was chasing
  (see R1/R2, below). Passed the plan's hard gate: AccuracyCoin 100%, the
  `cpu_interrupts_v2` trio, and the R5 DMC-DMA-span pin all held simultaneously
  flag-on.
- **beta.3 — the cycle-accurate warm reset.** Closes residual **R4**
  (`apu_reset/4017_written`): the APU now retains and reschedules the last
  `$4017` write on reset per the blargg spec, calibrated against the
  `4017_timing` printed delay window. Residual **R3** is reclassified as a test
  *harness* artifact (a stale-status re-detection bug), not a core defect, and
  closes on both build configurations once fixed.
- **beta.4 — THE PROMOTE.** The `mc-one-clock-v2` feature flag is deleted; the
  beta.1–beta.3 substrate becomes the **only** scheduler path, unconditionally.
  This is the release's designated breaking-behavior change. Net −151 lines
  (six caller-side counter mirrors, the legacy frame-counter reset path, the
  busless interrupt filler ticks, and the old DMA coherence fold all deleted
  as dead code). The 60-ROM commercial-oracle corpus was re-baselined
  (10 audio-hash-only diffs from a DMA-halt held-address correction matching
  the hardware references, 1 visually-inspected 0.2%-pixel diff) and holds
  **60/60**.
- **beta.5 — Vs. `DualSystem` dual-console support.** The four Vs. cabinet
  boards (Tennis, Mahjong, Wrecking Crew, Balloon Fight — two complete NES
  consoles sharing a WRAM mailbox and a cross-wired `$4016`/IRQ line) now
  construct and run as a genuine two-console pair via a new `Emu` front door.
  **Vs. Balloon Fight boots to a correct attract-mode screen** on both
  consoles, verified against the maintainer's own legitimately-owned MAME
  arcade romset (never committed). This is core-and-harness-only in this
  release — the frontend does not yet consume `Emu::Dual`, so the feature is
  unreachable from the shipped desktop/mobile UI pending a follow-up.
- **rc.1 — the format break + the architecture ADR.** `CPU_SNAPSHOT_VERSION`
  2→3, `save_state::FORMAT_VERSION` 1→2, `MOVIE_FORMAT_VERSION` 1→2 (warn, not
  reject — TAS movies remain replayable across the boundary; a new
  `recorded_before_v2_timebase()` helper flags the epoch). ADR 0029 formalizes
  the shipped one-clock model as the canonical architecture, superseding the
  dot-lockstep framing in `docs/architecture.md` and `docs/scheduler.md` (kept
  as marked historical content, not deleted).

## The R1/R2 residual — investigated, not silently dropped

The MMC3 IRQ-timing bracket that motivated the original per-dot-interleaving
diagnosis (`mmc3_test_2/4-scanline_timing`) did **not** close on the promoted
core. Per the plan's escape hatch, the maintainer authorized one bounded-effort
campaign (two sessions, 2026-07-02): four new levers were tested and falsified,
including confirming that the promoted `start_cycle`/`end_cycle` split-around-
the-access model already **is**, structurally, the missing piece Session-18
identified — it shipped as an emergent consequence of the one-clock collapse,
without anyone having connected it to that diagnosis until this campaign. The
mechanism finding: the ROM measures a *differential* interval invariant to any
consistent batch re-phasing, which is why 15+ prior phase/order levers (across
this and all previous attempts) were absorbed without effect. The axis is
**by-design-deferred beyond v2.0.0** with the full evidence trail and a
DO-NOT-RETRY list in ADR 0002 — not claimed closed, not silently abandoned.
Zero production-ROM impact; the four target brackets stay `#[ignore]`'d.

## Quality & determinism

**AccuracyCoin 100.00% (139/139)** on every beta and the final cut, `nestest`
0-diff against the Nintendulator golden log, the full CI gate matrix green
(fmt, the complete clippy feature matrix at `-D warnings`, `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D
warnings` rustdoc, the `no_std` chip-stack cross-compile, both wasm clippy
lines, and the `test-roms` accuracy suite). The every-cycle-bus-access model
was measured to add a sub-1%-of-frame-cost dummy read on formerly-busless
cycles — comfortably inside the 16.639 ms NTSC frame budget. Mapper breadth
stays frozen at **172 families** for this cut (no mapper work landed in the
v2.0.0 line; it resumes with the mobile-finalization train below).

## Upgrading

**Save-state slots and TAS movies from any v1.x release will not load
unmodified.** `.rns` slot files fail with a clear version-mismatch error rather
than silently misinterpreting stale bytes; `.rnm` TAS movies remain fully
replayable (button semantics and frame timing are unaffected) but are flagged
as pre-timebase so a caller can warn that the format's bit-identical
reproduction guarantee is unverified across the boundary. This is the
documented MAJOR-boundary policy (ADR 0003) exercised for the first time.
Everything else — ROM compatibility, controls, settings, cheats, achievements
progress — is unaffected.

## What's next

**v2.0.1 → v2.1.0 — the mobile finalization train.** The Android (v1.8.x) and
iOS (v1.9.x) apps were deliberately held on the old core until Timebase
landed; they now re-port onto v2.0.0 and launch together: **v2.0.1–v2.0.4**
finalize Android, **v2.0.5–v2.0.8** finalize iOS, **v2.0.9** is the joint
readiness gate, and **v2.1.0** is the joint Google Play + Apple App Store +
AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch (with the ad-supported $3.99 monetization tier
activating alongside it). Full plan: `to-dos/plans/v2.0.x-mobile-finalization-plan.md`.

## Install

Desktop binaries for Linux (x86_64), macOS (aarch64), and Windows (x86_64) are
attached below; the web build is live at <https://doublegate.github.io/RustyNES/>.
See `docs/architecture.md` and `docs/scheduler.md` for the new scheduling model,
and ADR 0002 / ADR 0028 / ADR 0029 for the full decision record. Dual-licensed
MIT OR Apache-2.0.

Built on the **v1.9.x "Workshop"** iOS TestFlight line, the **v1.8.x "Atlas"**
Android line, the **v1.10.0 "Arcade"** Libretro core release, and the
cycle-accurate v1.0.0 production core.
